title: Hard Times
author: Charles Dickens
content: “Now, what I want is, Facts. Teach these boys and girls nothing but Facts. Facts alone are wanted in life.”
action: Avoid reducing human experience to raw data.

title: Les Misérables
author: Victor Hugo
content: “Even the darkest night will end and the sun will rise.”

title: Notes from Underground
author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
content: “The best definition of man is: a creature that walks on two legs and is ungrateful.”
action: Account for human irrationality in your plans.

title: The Jungle
author: Upton Sinclair
content: “I aimed at the public's heart, and by accident I hit it in the stomach.”
action: Understand the visceral impact of your message.

title: Little Dorrit
author: Charles Dickens
content: “It is a law of nature that nothing is ever lost, but that all things are transformed.”
action: Look for the evolution within your losses.
